{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/6610","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/6610/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/6610/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/6610/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/6610","id":2718793026,"node_id":"I_kwDOAW4Y0s6iDX1C","number":6610,"title":"Enhancement: Update Kallisto wrapper adding option to save stdout as a dataset for MultiQC use","user":{"login":"jennaj","id":8999735,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5OTk3MzU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8999735?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj","html_url":"https://github.com/jennaj","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-12-04T21:09:15Z","updated_at":"2024-12-04T21:09:15Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"toolshed.g2.bx.psu.edu/repos/iuc/kallisto_quant/kallisto_quant/0.48.0+galaxy1

MultiQC will parse the **stdout** from Kallisto (only). The Galaxy wrapper for MultiQC seems ready to do this, but the file from Kallisto is not yet available. This request is to add an option to the Kallisto tool form to write that log as a dataset in the history for use in a workflow.

Xref
* Reported at https://help.galaxyproject.org/t/using-multiqc-with-kallisto/14075
* How the tool works https://github.com/MultiQC/MultiQC/issues/440#issuecomment-1024615275
* Prior PRs mentioned in https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/pull/1821

**Alternatively**: It would be super interesting to write a completely new tool that captures and writes out the stderr and/or stdout and/or info and/or description content -- from any dataset -- into the history as separate txt files. Maybe include a "stats" output with a checksum, timestamps, server/cluster details, anything else easily captured out of the technical metadata. Multi-use, we don't even need to know or care why. This probably needs a different ticket if anyone else likes the idea. stemming from 24.2 release testing process and PRs to address them"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-12-05T20:39:41Z","updated_at":"2024-12-05T20:39:41Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
Testing for https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19212

The listing of an account's workflows is not updated after an import until the user goes back several steps to "refresh" their listing. This means a user can't add other workflows as a sub-workflow until they are imported. I think that step should instead redirect to a pop-up "Please confirm. Do you want to import this workflow to add it as a subworkflow on the current canvas?". So, do this all in one step, right now it takes several clicks that are not very clear. 

I think these are really great functions, just need a bit of usage tuning! 



**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: (check <galaxy_url>/api/version if you don't know)
Commit: (run `git rev-parse HEAD` if you run this Galaxy server)

**Browser and Operating System**
Operating System: Windows, Linux, macOS
Browser: Firefox, Chrome, Chrome-based, Safari

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

Log into account. 
Click on Workflow in activity bar. 
Load up a workflow in the editor. 
Click on workflow in the side activity bar.
Use the default "introduction" tag to filter the listing

Enter the loop

1. Buttons for "Insert as sub-workflow" appear to be active no matter the import state of that workflow. Choose one from the published listing to work with for the next steps.
2. Clicking on **Insert** results in a canvas element displays a warning "user does not own specified item" 
3. Clicking on **Copy** results in "Please confirm. Warning this will add 39 new steps into your current workflow. You may want to consider using a subworkflow instead.". This works great! 
4. Clicking on **Import** (Import this workflow to edit) results in a success dialogue but _the listing of workflows is not updated_. 
5. Clicking on the **Insert** button again on the workflow that is listed loops back around and another items is added to the canvas with "user does not own specified item". I know why but the imported workflow didn't update in my list, so where to click is not clear.
6. Clicking on the activity bar "workflows" **twice** to get the updated listing (first time goes to full canvas, then the second time brings back the list) then the imported workflow is available, along with an Insert button that actually adds the sub-workflow.


Other odd behavior:

Can't save the workflow while any items with the "user does not own" are on the canvas. This makes it hard to know how to back out. Hopefully the user "knows" to click on workflow twice, or how to navigate the undo.



**Expected behavior**
Action that are not available are greyed out. The hover over tool tip has a suggestion of what to do next. 

**Related behavior**
1. On that list of prior actions for undo/redo -- any that didn't work and are unresolved (preventing a "save") should be highlighted in red. The same as the canvas item is in red. I'll ticket separately. 
2. After saving, I was confused about how to get back to Galaxy! Yes, lower bottom left but I miss the Home icon at the top. I'll ticket separately.

**Screenshots**

them"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-12-05T20:57:34Z","updated_at":"2024-12-05T20:57:34Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
Saving a workflow can be blocked by unresolved items on the workflow canvas.

If the user did something that is captured in the changes listing that is part of that save blocking, and the canvas item is in red with a warning, that action in the change list should also be highlighted in red.

Love the Changes redo/undo overall! And also like the save blocking for unstable changes. These are just some ideas to make the usage easier! 

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
This Galaxy server version is 25.0.dev0, and the web client was built on Wednesday Dec 4th 10:32:30 2024 GMT-8 .

**Browser and Operating System**
Operating System: Windows, Linux, **macOS**
Browser: Firefox, Chrome, Chrome-based, **Safari**

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to a workflow in the editor
2. Do something that prevents a save, such as adding a sub-workflow that is not owned ([example](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19265)). 
3. Try to save the workflow, get the message about the problem.
4. Go to your changes listing -- good luck! Which item is the problematic item isn't clear.
5. Presumably we know which that is, yes? Highlighting it would be great. Even better, also add that exact item to the warning, and tell the user to look under their Changes. This is how tool warnings appear during workflow imports -- maybe can reuse that same view here?
6. Any other problematic items that were already resolved by the user and not blocking the save should not be highlighted. 

**Expected behavior**
Change items blocking a save are clearly highlighted so the user can find and undo those.

**Screenshots**

![workflow-blocked-save1](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b4c1a0f8-2fbc-4212-aa24-f3b7647cfa9a)

![workflow-blocked-save2](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bee38966-106e-4a0d-93da-b2083d1028e8)


**Additional context**
Add any other context about the problem here.

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19269","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19269/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19269/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19269/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19269","id":2721466220,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc6iNkds","number":19269,"title":"Workflow: Navigation back to Galaxy after a save","user":{"login":"jennaj","id":8999735,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5OTk3MzU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8999735?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj","html_url":"https://github.com/jennaj","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-12-05T21:27:08Z","updated_at":"2024-12-05T21:27:09Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
After saving a workflow, getting "back to Galaxy" is a bit confusing.

Yes, there is the save and exit in the very bottom left corner of the new activity bar, but I missing the Home icon in the Masthead! 

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
This Galaxy server version is 25.0.dev0, and the web client was built on Wednesday Dec 4th 10:32:30 2024 GMT-8 .

**Browser and Operating System**
Operating System: Windows, Linux, **macOS**
Browser: Firefox, Chrome, Chrome-based, **Safari**

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Edit a workflow
2. Save it
3. Confused!
4. Find the tiny grey icon at the bottom left of the new activity bar. I already saved, but it does say "exit". 

**Expected behavior**
I was looking for the old Home (house) icon, since I always explicitly save, do another quick visual check of what I did eg workflow name and such, _then exit_. Maybe that is legacy behavior due to the Changes listing being available. I know this was discussed a lot in https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18166 and other places -- but I am just not used to not having the Home icon function super obvious. 

Ideas:

1. Add that back to the masthead, far left next to the Galaxy icon or maybe better, far to the right, before the window manager (scratchbook).
2. If the workflow is already saved -- maybe the icon in activity bar is changed to be the Home icon instead of the disc icon and text states Tools and Current History. 
3. Having both in the activity bar is also a possible. Grey out the Home icon if the workflow is not saved?
4. Move Notifications down to the bottom, since that is the least interesting item in the activity bar.
5. Place Save and Exit higher up in the activity bar. I could see it at the bottom under Download but down justified to "float" right above that first bar, where the others are top justified. I do think keeping it separate is a good idea to prevent accidental clicks.
6. Maybe this Save and Exit icon is made more obvious? An "active" orange color? Bolder lines?

**Screenshots**
![how-do-I-get-back-to-Galaxy](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a8badc53-8330-4f51-a5cb-aaf8fe1c1a0a)


**Additional context**
Add any other context about the problem here.

the bug**
Saving a workflow can be blocked by unresolved items on the workflow canvas.

If the user did something that is captured in the changes listing that is part of that save blocking, and the canvas item is in red with a warning, that action in the change list should also be highlighted in red.

Love the Changes redo/undo overall! And also like the save blocking for unstable changes. These are just some ideas to make the usage easier! 

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
This Galaxy server version is 25.0.dev0, and the web client was built on Wednesday Dec 4th 10:32:30 2024 GMT-8 .

**Browser and Operating System**
Operating System: Windows, Linux, **macOS**
Browser: Firefox, Chrome, Chrome-based, **Safari**

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to a workflow in the editor
2. Do something that prevents a save, such as adding a sub-workflow that is not owned ([example](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19265)). 
3. Try to save the workflow, get the message about the problem.
4. Go to your changes listing -- good luck! Which item is the problematic item isn't clear.
5. Presumably we know which that is, yes? Highlighting it would be great. Even better, also add that exact item to the warning, and tell the user to look under their Changes. This is how tool warnings appear during workflow imports -- maybe can reuse that same view here?
6. Any other problematic items that were already resolved by the user and not blocking the save should not be highlighted. 

**Expected behavior**
Change items blocking a save are clearly highlighted so the user can find and undo those.

**Screenshots**

![workflow-blocked-save1](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b4c1a0f8-2fbc-4212-aa24-f3b7647cfa9a)

![workflow-blocked-save2](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bee38966-106e-4a0d-93da-b2083d1028e8)


**Additional context**
Add any other context about the problem here.

the bug**
Testing for https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19212

The listing of an account's workflows is not updated after an import until the user goes back several steps to "refresh" their listing. This means a user can't add other workflows as a sub-workflow until they are imported. I think that step should instead redirect to a pop-up "Please confirm. Do you want to import this workflow to add it as a subworkflow on the current canvas?". So, do this all in one step, right now it takes several clicks that are not very clear. 

I think these are really great functions, just need a bit of usage tuning! 



**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: (check <galaxy_url>/api/version if you don't know)
Commit: (run `git rev-parse HEAD` if you run this Galaxy server)

**Browser and Operating System**
Operating System: Windows, Linux, **macOS**
Browser: Firefox, Chrome, Chrome-based, **Safari**

**To Reproduce**
This Galaxy server version is 25.0.dev0, and the web client was built on Wednesday Dec 4th 10:32:30 2024 GMT-8 .

Log into account. 
Click on Workflow in activity bar. 
Load up a workflow in the editor. 
Click on workflow in the side activity bar.
Use the default "introduction" tag to filter the listing

Enter the loop

1. Buttons for "Insert as sub-workflow" appear to be active no matter the import state of that workflow. Choose one from the published listing to work with for the next steps.
2. Clicking on **Insert** results in a canvas element displays a warning "user does not own specified item" 
3. Clicking on **Copy** results in "Please confirm. Warning this will add 39 new steps into your current workflow. You may want to consider using a subworkflow instead.". This works great! 
4. Clicking on **Import** (Import this workflow to edit) results in a success dialogue but _the listing of workflows is not updated_. 
5. Clicking on the **Insert** button again on the workflow that is listed loops back around and another items is added to the canvas with "user does not own specified item". I know why but the imported workflow didn't update in my list, so where to click is not clear.
6. Clicking on the activity bar "workflows" **twice** to get the updated listing (first time goes to full canvas, then the second time brings back the list) then the imported workflow is available, along with an Insert button that actually adds the sub-workflow.


Other odd behavior:

Can't save the workflow while any items with the "user does not own" are on the canvas. This makes it hard to know how to back out. Hopefully the user "knows" to click on workflow twice, or how to navigate the undo.



**Expected behavior**
Action that are not available are greyed out. The hover over tool tip has a suggestion of what to do next. 

**Related behavior**
1. On that list of prior actions for undo/redo -- any that didn't work and are unresolved (preventing a "save") should be highlighted in red. The same as the canvas item is in red. https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19266
2. After saving, I was confused about how to get back to Galaxy! Yes, lower bottom left but I miss the Home icon at the top. https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19269

**Screenshots**

request"},{"id":928750545,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw5Mjg3NTA1NDU=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/idc/labels/question","name":"question","color":"d876e3","default":true,"description":"Further requested"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-07T19:20:37Z","updated_at":"2024-11-07T19:20:37Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Meta concept**

The label used to designate genomic information across data providers can differ, even for the same underlying information. UCSC has addressed this need with the concept of **aliases**. The IDC would benefit from leveraging this information in Galaxy-adjacent projects and applications.

**Example: mm39**

Source file `https://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/goldenPath/mm39/database/chromAlias.txt.gz`

Shared history with that file loaded -> https://test.galaxyproject.org/u/jenjackson/h/mouse-alias-table

_Screenshots_

![Screen Shot 2024-11-07 at 11 08 33 AM](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/657a305b-cb25-4c8e-b42b-4b8b072273ce)

![Screen Shot 2024-11-07 at 11 13 31 AM](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6a3cb62f-ec5c-48c1-8c7d-14dfa2b7f2a2)


If this file had more rows added, specifically the UCSC source with the root dbkey, the IDC could consume the data, and Galaxy could use it for file fetching on workflow forms, external applications (link outs and data pushes), and possibly implicit conversion of data at runtime for tool consumption. There are likely other practical uses such as improved error trapping and automated use suggestions e.g. chatGXY training data.

This is hard but seems possible, and maybe required for long term scaling goals across the project. toolshed.g2.bx.psu.edu/repos/iuc/snpeff/snpEff_build_gb/5.2+galaxy0

Recent changes to turning off the db check seems to have problems. The warning box in blue goes away then pops back up and prevents the tool from submitting. Any server, several different test and real data runs. Has anyone been able to get this to work after the late Oct updates?

Workaround for end users:
1. Create your snpeffdb with SnpEff build 4.3+T.galaxy6 first
2. Then use SnpEff eff 4.3+T.galaxy2
3. Note that you might need to submit the job a few times to get it to go through

Reported at https://help.galaxyproject.org/t/snpeff-database-compatibility/13923/5


One of the test histories: https://usegalaxy.eu/u/jenj/h/test-snpeff-build

Screenshots: notice how the message will repeat and populate again if you click on the submit button a few times with v 5.2. Doing the repeat submission will allow a 4.3 job to eventually submit and run. 

![Screen Shot 2024-11-18 at 10 28 39 AM](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4d46cf7d-925b-49fc-b65f-b763f4a26bec)


![Screen Shot 2024-11-18 at 10 39 21 AM](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c05e8c4a-7f3f-42dd-90c5-a8d30f33f814)

the bug**
Cosmetic items with the workflow invocation listing.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
The Galaxy Server is running version [24.1.3.dev0 ](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/release_24.1/releases/24.1_announce_user.html), and the web client was built on Monday Sep 30th 7:27:28 2024 GMT-7 .

**Browser and Operating System**
Operating System: Windows, Linux, **macOS**
Browser: Firefox, **Chrome**, Chrome-based, **Safari**

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Have some workflow invocations!
2. Click on Data -> Workflow invocations (or get the listing from the activity bar)
3. Notice a few cosmetic items. Yes, the user can adjust the panel width but it would be nice to make it slicker automatically if possible (without creating a super tall
expa
ded
vie
w).

**Word wrapping seems odd**
Words in the comments section will split mid-word between lines. Could this be a smarter split? Hyphenate or just push to the next line if there are not enough chars? Not simple but maybe we can be smart about it. For published workflows, this doesn't look great.
![word-wrapping](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/31b6f213-2f5e-4ad9-b96e-c696300679dc)

**Icon covers text**
"Active" invocation indicator overwrites comment text.

the bug**
More ways to get notifications about workflows, in a meta way instead of the workflow editor per tool method.

If people are running workflows created by someone else, going into the editor to adjust a final tools seems too tedious. Maybe we can come up ways to make this simpler. 

Starting ideas

**At workflow runtime**

Put this under the gear icon as another customization? 

![Screen Shot 2024-10-07 at 11 17 19 AM](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a93dd81f-39f1-476d-b8aa-64c8c01d6178)

**As a global user preference**

As a meta User -> Preferences setting. Meaning, a user can choose to get a notification about any workflow run by default (still leaving the option to disable in the gear icon per execution). 

At a minimum, a card with the option to get a workflow invocation status notification whenever one "completes" seems useful. E.g. that invocation isn't processing any scheduled jobs anymore for any reason. 

Maybe we can think of other stages to capture? 
* An invocation "completed job scheduling" -- seems useful since that can take a while for the really large workflows. 
* An invocation's "sub-workflows" versus "main" completed -- maybe set for all or none as a fine tuning option.

If these had meaningful notification titles, that would be a great way to track progress via an email thread, without that person needing to do anything in the editor itself. 

![Screen Shot 2024-10-07 at 11 22 12 AM](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/57ace2a0-09a4-4493-a5bd-7ab946044eef)

Example use case about this at the forum:  https://help.galaxyproject.org/t/how-to-set-notification-when-job-workflow-complete/13613

If this is covered in another ticket, please consolidate. I searched but maybe missed it. Thanks!

Note: this tool is somewhat difficult to find with a tool panel search. It is a good example of when a common short name for a tool isn't included in the usual tool xml places, and the first word in the tool's long name is non-specific (e.g. "remove"). "RUV" or "PRPS" is how the publication, and many users, will search for this one. I'm tagging his with "site/*" in hopes we'll find/use the example with future tool panel searching dev updates.

That way of using the tool does not actually work for people. The tool needs the full "database" to perform the processing. This seems to be true with the command-line version of the tool, too, so not a "galaxy specific" thing, just how it works.

If the custom file is provided, the statistics and outputs both suffer. This is confusing for users. I am not sure if this ever worked, so I think we should remove that custom option from even being possible on the tool form since what is the intended use?

If I am just missing something, super happy to be corrected! Maybe some situations will work, some won't, and we can clarify that in the tool form help better? I thought I asked about this already but maybe never actually made the ticket. Please merge into the right place as needed. :) Thanks!

artifacts","user":{"login":"jennaj","id":8999735,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5OTk3MzU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8999735?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj","html_url":"https://github.com/jennaj","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":241204064,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNDEyMDQwNjQ=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/area/UI-UX","name":"area/UI-UX","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":null},{"id":242034312,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNDIwMzQzMTI=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/feature-request","name":"feature-request","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-09-10T22:02:22Z","updated_at":"2024-09-10T22:02:22Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Currently, the "transfer by URL" fails over at https://view.qiime2.org for known reasons per @mvdbeek.

But @bernt-matthias thinks we can maybe configure a .loc file to make hosting by URL possible.

This ticket is to track the end-user hurdle, and a potential solution.

The operation otherwise requires a Galaxy dataset download, then upload to the https://view.qiime2.org site. Exploring data there is recommended by the Qiime2 authors the primary method an end-user can visually inspect/explore what is actually inside of a qiime2 artifact/viz.

Other ideas: could the function of the https://view.qiime2.org website be bundled as a Galaxy tool? Could that be accessed by clicking on the eye icon, similar to how a peek view for compressed BAMs is displayed as a SAM? Or maybe better, as a dedicated Visualize tool?

Qimme artifacts are a data structure with some metadata (qzv) plus a list of content similar to a collection (qza) ... and are somewhat opaque in Galaxy. Enabling some kind of viz would be a start for making the usage easier.

Could the file headers be written out? Maybe as an optional yes/no toggle on the form? Default is no (how it works now).

Request https://help.galaxyproject.org/t/dnadiff-output-files/13390/5?u=jennaj

> Since we’re at it, I would like to point out another small flaw: in the result files of DNAdiff, when I view them within the Galaxy web page, I can see headers for each column (ex. 1.11+galaxy1","user":{"login":"jennaj","id":8999735,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5OTk3MzU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8999735?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj","html_url":"https://github.com/jennaj","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-09-16T22:41:04Z","updated_at":"2024-09-16T22:41:04Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"The updated version of MultiQC toolshed.g2.bx.psu.edu/repos/iuc/multiqc/multiqc/1.24.1+galaxy0 has trouble parsing certain data lines into a custom content table, specifically any that start with a hash # character.

The prior version of MultiQC toolshed.g2.bx.psu.edu/repos/iuc/multiqc/multiqc/1.11+galaxy1 was either able to strip out the hash # and leading space OR those were ignored (?) during processing. Meaning, those values did not trigger a failure, and were not displayed in the final output HTML table either.

I can't tell if this change is inside MultiQC itself or the Galaxy wrapper. MultiQC did seem to make changes to support more custom plot types .. maybe a hash is now a reserved character, or the yaml content checks are stricter? 

Test history with full details: https://usegalaxy.eu/u/jenj/h/bug-quast-tabular-report-into-multiqc-custom-table

Reported at: https://help.galaxyproject.org/t/updated-version-of-multiqc-1-24-1-galaxy0/13431

> **Workaround for Galaxy end-users**
> * Symptom: trouble parsing certain data lines into a custom output.
> * What to do: inspect the input file format and try to simplify. 
> * Is a particular line or file the problem? find clues in your job logs! click into the [i-icon ](https://training.galaxyproject.org/training-material/faqs/galaxy/datasets_icons.html)and review.
> * A guide is available here: https://multiqc.info/docs/custom_content/","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/6335/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/6335/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

Contains the same test job run at ORG (this bug) and EU (success). AU also has success. Not sure why these differ, maybe due to some custom config at ORG?

Green results, some outputs populated, some empty (the actual count files). Impacts all tool versions I've tested, including the most current toolshed.g2.bx.psu.edu/repos/iuc/stringtie/stringtie/2.2.3+galaxy0

**Example ORG**

Job API ID | bbd44e69cb8906b5b1dc488c5743e548 (60570475)

stderr
```
/usr/local/bin/prepDE.py:72: SyntaxWarning: invalid escape sequence '\-'
  RE_COVERAGE=re.compile('cov "([\-\+\d\.]+)"')
/usr/local/bin/prepDE.py:75: SyntaxWarning: invalid escape sequence '\-'
  RE_GFILE=re.compile('\-G\s*(\S+)') #assume filepath without spaces..
```

Impact: Any NCBI query tool that accepts accessions as input. These can be entered as individual accessions or a list of accessions (one per line). When whitespace is added to the tool form, or within a list, this causes the tools to fail. Trailing blank line(s) can also lead to avoidable failures. 

Proposed fix: Strip any extra whitespace, empty newlines, or lines that contain only whitespace before making the query against NCBI. Any whitespace is not important to retain as far as I know, and cleaning up data seems like a practical "make it easier to use" change for these tools. Doesn't come up too often, but enough to address. **Papercut?**

Example of the error in the command line for a failure:

`acc='SRR1234567	'`

What the `stderr` reports:

> stderr
> /galaxy-repl/main/jobdir/XXX/XXX/XXXXXXXX/tool_script.sh: line 60: [: SRR1234567: unary operator expected

differential-isoform-expression","user":{"login":"jennaj","id":8999735,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5OTk3MzU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8999735?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj","html_url":"https://github.com/jennaj","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":450564804,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw0NTA1NjQ4MDQ=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/training-material/labels/discussion","name":"discussion","color":"0000cc","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-08-07T01:33:29Z","updated_at":"2024-08-07T01:33:30Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/training-material/blob/main/topics/transcriptomics/tutorials/differential-isoform-expression/tutorial.md

Hi all -- The workflow in this tutorial doesn't play well with the tutorial data. It requires that the student has a list of the element identifiers for the control samples as a distinct input text list, and that list isn't included in the workflow datasets, plus I think it would be better as a derived filter list, the same as the hands-on.

**What I changed:** Since the workflow already includes simple inputs for the factor level naming, those can be reused in the regular expression filtering of element identifiers (for the collection splitting step) with the addition of just two tools and tiny related changes. All of those mirror the hands-on tools/steps. 

![MODIFIED-FOR-GTN_differential-isoform-expression](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f312dc73-7e92-4447-9b06-f8a423e32b0d)

I've created a sample modified version of the workflow with those adjustments.
* Input data https://usegalaxy.eu/u/jenj/h/gtn-differential-isoform-expression
* Workflow output https://usegalaxy.eu/u/jenj/h/genome-wide-alternative-splicing-analysis-human-modified-for-gtn
* Workflow https://usegalaxy.eu/u/jenj/w/genome-wide-alternative-splicing-analysis-human

While this tutorial is labeled as advanced, it assumes quite a bit of knowledge about Galaxy and workflow editing to get it to work. I think we should update it unless there is some other use of it where the proposed changes would conflict (?).

Thoughts? ping @pavanvidem @gallardoalba @lldelisle ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/training-material/issues/5208/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/training-material/issues/5208/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

Hi all -- The workflow in this tutorial doesn't play well with the tutorial data. It requires that the student has a list of the element identifiers for the control samples as a distinct input text list, and that list isn't included in the workflow datasets, plus I think it would be better as a derived filter list, the same as the hands-on.

**What I changed:** Since the workflow already includes simple inputs for the factor level naming, those can be reused in the regular expression filtering of element identifiers (for the collection splitting step) with the addition of just two tools and tiny related changes. All of those mirror the hands-on tools/steps. 

![MODIFIED-FOR-GTN_differential-isoform-expression](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f312dc73-7e92-4447-9b06-f8a423e32b0d)

I've created a sample modified version of the workflow with those adjustments.
* Input data https://usegalaxy.eu/u/jenj/h/gtn-differential-isoform-expression
* Workflow output https://usegalaxy.eu/u/jenj/h/genome-wide-alternative-splicing-analysis-human-modified-for-gtn
* Workflow https://usegalaxy.eu/u/jenj/w/genome-wide-alternative-splicing-analysis-human

While this tutorial is labeled as advanced, it assumes quite a bit of knowledge about Galaxy and workflow editing to get it to work. I think we should update it unless there is some other use of it where the proposed changes would conflict (?).

Overall -- we could consider using group tags to filter, instead of the whole "filter list identifiers by the way the fastq files are named". It makes the workflow very specific to the tutorial, and not as reusable. Scientist are more likely to have files without custom naming, or even SRR identifiers. Maybe upload the control + treat collections, give them group tags, merge together for some of the processing, then split for other processing. I can help if we want to do this.

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18739","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18739/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18739/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18739/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18739","id":2487693519,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc6URzDP","number":18739,"title":"Feature request: Send output to new History (top of tool form)","user":{"login":"jennaj","id":8999735,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5OTk3MzU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8999735?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj","html_url":"https://github.com/jennaj","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-08-26T20:15:56Z","updated_at":"2024-08-26T20:15:56Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
I sometimes want to rerun a job and have the output sent to a new history. 

I also sometimes want to filter the history (tag or keyword or related-items) and move that bundle into a new history to do targeted work.

In short, to start over with just parts of my original working data. This is likely a common way to do research work. Let's make it easier by reusing functions already available in other parts of the app.

I can think of two entry points we don't have, and I think we should do both! 
1. Include a "Send to new History" option at the top of the tool form (similar to the Workflow execution). We could consolidate both the data storage location + which history location to write to into a Gear icon on the **Tool form**. I'm ticketing that here.
2. Add "Copy Datasets" to the Operate on Multiple Datasets listing of options. This was already requested here https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/8798. Not sure of the status? 

Drag and drop in Multi-history view is great, but doesn't capture dataset associations like a filter or a rerun. I automatically go to these two other UI places _every time_ I haven't used Galaxy for a few days!

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
The Galaxy Server is running version [24.1.2.dev0 ](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/release_24.1/releases/24.1_announce_user.html), and the web client was built on Monday Aug 26th 6:23:57 2024 GMT-7 .

**Browser and Operating System**
Operating System: Windows, Linux, **macOS**
Browser: Firefox, **Chrome**, Chrome-based, Safari

**Request**

Consolidate the single storage icon into a **gear icon** with both storage and output destinations. A single-click is important here.
* Reusing the original history name in an auto-generated name will help with provenance. "Copied from original-history-name".
* All inputs copied over, then new outputs written to new history.
* Post back a green confirmation banner with the new history name and a link. Similar to other functions (history import, workflow invocation)

**Place this workflow invocation gear icon**

![workflow-invocation](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7b6ea5da-874f-4471-8f2f-207a061e141b)


**At the top of the tool form, consolidating storage and output destinations, instead of only offering storage**

![tool-form-invocation](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/50f105c5-10ce-4c9f-b1cb-bdc39f9e3d96)

the bug**
The MSA viewer will not load at UseGalaxy.org. It will load at other UseGalaxy servers.
Interestingly, you can name and save the still-loading visualization, plus generate a share link.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
The Galaxy Server is running version [24.1.2.dev0 ](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/release_24.1/releases/24.1_announce_user.html), and the web client was built on Thursday Jul 18th 1:13:13 2024 GMT-7 .

**Browser and Operating System**
Operating System: Windows, Linux, **macOS**
Browser: Firefox, **Chrome**, Chrome-based, **Safari**

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to a fasta with multiple sequences
2. Click on chart icon
3. Select the msa viewer
4. See error: continually loading

Shared history with fasta: https://usegalaxy.org/u/jen-at-org/h/copy-of-test-charts-for-msa-viewer
Shared stalled visualization: https://usegalaxy.org/u/jen-at-org/v/hello-msa

Output options: Output aligned/unaligned queries to separate file
* aligned + unaligned = both written (good!)
* aligned only = none written (should be aligned file, only, but nothing extra is produced)
* unaligned only = both written (should be unaligned file, only, not both)

Tool form also has a typo in that option:
![diamond-typo](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/29d5c1e3-f6f4-4040-84b0-e672a909457b)

Problem seems to be here. Maybe @abretaud or @wm75 can help?

https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/blame/main/tools/diamond/diamond.xml#L298-L319

Thanks!

CONVERTER_gff_to_interval_index_0

Convert GFF to Interval Index needs more memory to process human sized genomes.
Currently at default. `--mem=2765`
500k line GTF = processes ok 
1.9M lines =  fails .. and some versions can be 4.5M lines

> Workaround for end-users
> * Expand the red error dataset. If you see a message about memory usage, you are impacted.
> * The solution for now is to try at another server: UseGalaxy.eu or UseGalaxy.org.au

Test history https://usegalaxy.org/u/jen-galaxyproject/h/imported-from-archive-training-16s-rrna-sequencing-with-mothur-main-tutorial-imported-from-url----classifyseqs-purges-tax-input

Test workflow -- same as GTN Tutorial https://training.galaxyproject.org/training-material/topics/microbiome/tutorials/mothur-miseq-sop/workflows/

What was done:

1. Load up tutorial inputs
2. Run the workflow at EU
3. Export History from EU > Import to ORG (history), Download WF from EU > Upload to ORG
4. Run the workflow at ORG
5. See the bug .. the tax input is purged.

This usage makes use of the converter tool CONVERTER_ref_to_seq_taxomony but when I run that directly in a different test history, it works fine. https://usegalaxy.org/u/jen-galaxyproject/h/test-ref-to-seq

I moved the WF from EU > ORG but this also happens if the workflow is directly loaded from GTN. This was a cross test to see if the WF was the problem or not -- seems not, works at EU but not at ORG.

Same happened in Dec 2023 but it seemed like a fluke, see https://usegalaxy.org/u/jen-galaxyproject/h/training-16s-rrna-sequencing-with-mothur-main-tutorial-3 

Did work Ok in Nov 2023 https://usegalaxy.org/u/jen-galaxyproject/h/training-16s-rrna-sequencing-with-mothur-main-tutorial-2

So something happened between Nov 27 2023 and Dec 13 2023 to impact this tool. No successful runs since then.

Started up another just to see what happens https://usegalaxy.org/u/jen-galaxyproject/h/training-16s-rrna-sequencing-with-mothur-main-tutorial-imported-from-url-imported-from-uploaded-file","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-tools/issues/739/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-tools/issues/739/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

working"}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-05-02T20:00:30Z","updated_at":"2024-05-10T19:34:40Z","closed_at":"2024-05-10T19:34:40Z","author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"tool_id=toolshed.g2.bx.psu.edu/repos/iuc/mothur_classify_seqs/mothur_classify_seqs/1.39.5.0

Test history https://usegalaxy.org/u/jen-galaxyproject/h/imported-from-archive-training-16s-rrna-sequencing-with-mothur-main-tutorial-imported-from-url----classifyseqs-purges-tax-input

Test workflow -- same as GTN Tutorial https://training.galaxyproject.org/training-material/topics/microbiome/tutorials/mothur-miseq-sop/workflows/

What was done:

1. Load up tutorial inputs
2. Run the workflow at EU
3. Export History from EU > Import to ORG (history), Download WF from EU > Upload to ORG
4. Run the workflow at ORG
5. See the bug .. the tax input is purged.

This usage makes use of the converter tool CONVERTER_ref_to_seq_taxomony but when I run that directly in a different test history, it works fine. https://usegalaxy.org/u/jen-galaxyproject/h/test-ref-to-seq

I moved the WF from EU > ORG but this also happens if the workflow is directly loaded from GTN. This was a cross test to see if the WF was the problem or not -- seems not, works at EU but not at ORG.

Same happened in Dec 2023 but it seemed like a fluke, see https://usegalaxy.org/u/jen-galaxyproject/h/training-16s-rrna-sequencing-with-mothur-main-tutorial-3 

Did work Ok in Nov 2023 https://usegalaxy.org/u/jen-galaxyproject/h/training-16s-rrna-sequencing-with-mothur-main-tutorial-2

Test history https://usegalaxy.org/u/jen-galaxyproject/h/imported-from-archive-training-16s-rrna-sequencing-with-mothur-main-tutorial-imported-from-url----classifyseqs-purges-tax-input

Test workflow -- same as GTN Tutorial https://training.galaxyproject.org/training-material/topics/microbiome/tutorials/mothur-miseq-sop/workflows/

What was done:

1. Load up tutorial inputs
2. Run the workflow at EU
3. Export History from EU > Import to ORG (history), Download WF from EU > Upload to ORG
4. Run the workflow at ORG
5. See the bug .. the tax input is purged.

This usage makes use of the converter tool CONVERTER_ref_to_seq_taxomony but when I run that directly in a different test history, it works fine. https://usegalaxy.org/u/jen-galaxyproject/h/test-ref-to-seq

I moved the WF from EU > ORG but this also happens if the workflow is directly loaded from GTN. This was a cross test to see if the WF was the problem or not -- seems not, works at EU but not at ORG.

Same happened in Dec 2023 but it seemed like a fluke, see https://usegalaxy.org/u/jen-galaxyproject/h/training-16s-rrna-sequencing-with-mothur-main-tutorial-3 

Did work Ok in Nov 2023 https://usegalaxy.org/u/jen-galaxyproject/h/training-16s-rrna-sequencing-with-mothur-main-tutorial-2

So something happened between Nov 27 2023 and Dec 13 2023 to impact this tool. No successful runs since then.

the bug**
Menu items can be written over with the "click to select" message. 

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
The Galaxy Server is running version [24.0.3.dev0 ](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/release_24.0/releases/24.0_announce_user.html), and the web client was built on Tuesday May 14th 7:04:11 2024 GMT-7 .

**Browser and Operating System**
Operating System: macOS
Browser: Chrome

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go into the workflow editor
2. For a tool when choosing options, click into a select menu with a listing
3. Notice the content is written over with default width. The part covered up can be the important part!
4. Expand the width of that panel (if you know how to..): items can still be written over for some cases

**Expected behavior**
Narrow views: toggle to a context-dependent curser or icon that prompts the user to "click" instead of the longer text message that is covering up the items in the list.

**Screenshots**

Default on my browser

![Screen Shot 2024-05-14 at 3 00 23 PM](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/8999735/5ccaa65f-8268-443d-b8e3-f9407018a1e6)

Width adjusted some. Yes, this could go a bit wider but is that the best solution?

![Screen Shot 2024-05-14 at 2 54 07 PM](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/8999735/67c2270f-ca3d-438c-8bbe-c8d4b82ab0fb)


**Additional context**
Screenshots are from one of the first tutorials some people might try. 10x scRNA  > https://training.galaxyproject.org/training-material/topics/single-cell/tutorials/scrna-preprocessing-tenx/tutorial.html

The workflow requires one tune-up after import. But the experience seems.. not so smooth.

Some kind of toggle to an icon or arrow (?) when space is tight, that doesn't overwrite the menu items, would be nicer. 

Please consolidate if this is a known","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18150/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18150/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

This will address many of the prior failures. 

> submit_native_specification | --nodes=1 --ntasks=3 --mem=**12288** --time=36:00:00 --partition=tpv

For anyone who still encounters jobs [failing for memory resources](https://training.galaxyproject.org/training-material/faqs/galaxy/troubleshooting_excess_memory.html), and who have confirmed there is not a data content or reference data issue (do check! you ask a [question at our forum](https://help.galaxyproject.org/) for community help), you will either need to **reduce the BAM size** (search the tool panel with `filter bam` and consider these options for scientific reasons too: an appropriate minimum mapQ, intact proper pairs, primary hits, exclude unmapped) or you can **try at a different public Galaxy server** -> [UseGalaxy.eu](UseGalaxy.eu) or [UseGalaxy.org.au](UseGalaxy.org.au) are both good choices.

generated","user":{"login":"jennaj","id":8999735,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5OTk3MzU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8999735?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj","html_url":"https://github.com/jennaj","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":276600485,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNzY2MDA0ODU=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/triage","name":"triage","color":"fbca04","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2020-02-15T00:58:40Z","updated_at":"2024-05-22T20:41:53Z","closed_at":"2024-05-22T20:41:53Z","author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"This was a problem in a prior release and was corrected. It seems to be back. Reproducible across servers. Not Python2/3 related from what I can tell (org running py3, eu running py2, both has the same issue. 

How to reproduce. Isn't 100% of the time, but if share 3 histories new, chances are at least one (if not all) will have the problem.

1. Generate a history share link the first time. 
  *  The updated status is not saved/persistent.
  *  Go to _User > Histories_ to check, or navigate back to the _Share or Publish_ form.
2. Go back into _Share or Publish_, toggle the share by URL link off then on, is saved/persistent. 

Note: Sharing a Workflow the first time always seems to work. As does share a History with another Galaxy user (email). So the issue seems specific to sharing history by URL, and just when the link is first initialized.

cc @dannon - did you correct this before? I can't find the PR. > Hi, I am trying to run the RepeatModeler tool on a ~1.5 GB genome using the public [usegalaxy.org](http://usegalaxy.org) server. I’ve tried running this job several times, but each time I get the error message that the job terminated because it ran longer than the allowed job run time. Just wondering if there is a way to multithread this job in the [usegalaxy.org](http://usegalaxy.org) server to speed up the run time, or if there are any other ways to get this job to finish?

**Workaround**: Run the job at the UseGalaxy.eu server instead for now since the default runtime will be longer

cases**

Sharing involves links, and is a distinctly useful feature of Galaxy. Including those associations in an accessible, and integrated, view directly on primary artifacts would be useful and _alert users that these connections exist_. History tags are super useful .. but don't really link things together outside of the Data > History view. Siloed.

General 

* I want to capture the share link already created for a history quickly. 🔗  icon
* I want to share or unshare quickly. Add in 🔒  with a tool tip*?
* I want to change the status from private/shared/published. 📜  with a tool tip*?
* I want to know if history is shared with other people by account email. 🫂 w tool tip*
* **Discussion point**: I'm following a GTN tutorial and adding in the URL to the comments (as a reminder) is NOT converted to a hyperlink. Recognize text and convert to 🎓 ? Could be more than one..
* **Discussion point**: I want to associated other links that are known public Galaxy properties: Hub, Github, Ghelp, Matrix

Histories

* Other histories related to this history. Common tags?
* Could be history links _at other UseGalaxy servers_ (not tag based, but user entered)

Workflows or Workflow Invocations

* I want to know that this history is associated with a WF invocation (reverse of WF inv link to a history)
* I want to associate a workflow with a history. 
    * Maybe several WFs. 
    * Provide an :flowchart: icon that redirects to the Workflow listing view (same as if clicked on masthead) but **pre-filtered** 
* Have workflow connections _automatically populated_ if they wrote output to that history
   * definitely for histories created via "send output to new history"
* **Discussion point**: How to handle workflow _version_ with the redirect

I’ve tried running this job several times, but each time I get the error message that the job terminated because it ran longer than the allowed job run time. Just wondering if there is a way to multithread this job in the [usegalaxy.org](http://usegalaxy.org) server to speed up the run time, or if there are any other ways to get this job to finish?

Workaround: Run the job at the UseGalaxy.eu server instead for now since the default runtime will be longer

https://help.galaxyproject.org/t/no-link-for-ucsc-to-visualise-datasets/12260/

From UCSC. This would either be in addition to the ucsc.builds.txt
You will also need all genbank-ids that we have here on our side… as a text file: https://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/hubs/UCSC_GI.assemblyHubList.txt

What might work
1. Use all of the links/tools as they are now, and (optionally) supplement the builds list.

Known wrinkles so far
1. The UCSC browser serves the dbkey/database from the Table browser with a specific identifier format that differs slightly from the root identifier, and it is current server specific. This is being looked at (followup discussion could be here).
2. Use like a custom genome? Users source the fasta, indexed at runtime, but can use the pre-existing database keys? Seems like a yes unless we want to generate indexes.

Installing a local Galaxy is complicated. Could we add more to trap a few obvious mixups with `run.sh` and `common_startup.sh`? The message now doesn't come up with _some_ use cases. Maybe add a few more sanity checks with a human-readable message parsed out at a top level?

For an example, see https://help.galaxyproject.org/t/python-command-not-found-on-local-galaxy-istance/11763

","user":{"login":"jennaj","id":8999735,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5OTk3MzU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8999735?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj","html_url":"https://github.com/jennaj","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":180024669,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxODAwMjQ2Njk=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/kind/bug","name":"kind/bug","color":"84b6eb","default":false,"description":null},{"id":241204064,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNDEyMDQwNjQ=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/area/UI-UX","name":"area/UI-UX","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":null},{"id":6656638981,"node_id":"LA_kwDOAdw-Nc8AAAABjMRABQ","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/release-testing-24.0","name":"release-testing-24.0","color":"D3BEFF","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2023-12-18T18:43:56Z","updated_at":"2024-03-07T19:10:40Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
1. `Copy` function on User > Histories Shared with Me doesn't do anything (UI "blinks" once quickly, but history not copied into account)
2. `Unshare` function on same view results in a "grid refresh" error and when UI reloaded, not removed from listing
3. `View` works fine

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: ({"version_major":"23.2","version_minor":"rc1"}

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. From account1, share a history with account2 by email address using History > Share or Publish > Share History with Individual Users
2. In account2, attempt to `Copy` that history, or `Unshare`it.

I can share a history with whoever works on this if they need more context.

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17735","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17735/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17735/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17735/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17735","id":2187124935,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc6CXODH","number":17735,"title":"Collections: which datasets are actually inside a collection is mixed up between views/actions","user":{"login":"jennaj","id":8999735,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5OTk3MzU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8999735?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj","html_url":"https://github.com/jennaj","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":241204064,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNDEyMDQwNjQ=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/area/UI-UX","name":"area/UI-UX","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":null},{"id":295111381,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyOTUxMTEzODE=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/area/dataset-collections","name":"area/dataset-collections","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":null},{"id":6656638981,"node_id":"LA_kwDOAdw-Nc8AAAABjMRABQ","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/release-testing-24.0","name":"release-testing-24.0","color":"D3BEFF","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-03-14T19:31:50Z","updated_at":"2024-03-14T19:31:51Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
The datasets included in a collection appear to be a mix of the original datasets and the datasets copied into the collection depending on the view -- Specifically: which you view nested inside the _collection when expanded_ in the history (original files added to the collection) differs from which metadata is inherited/displayed on the _collapsed collection_ (actual files inside the collection). 

In practical use: if any of the hidden datasets are manipulated (active/deleted state changed, datatype changed) what can be viewed in the history panel is a mix of those states! This can impact how users curate data in the hidden panel (it happens..), how tool forms consume the collections, and using some of the state change functions (in particular, when clicking on the "eye" icon for a dataset in an expanded collection via the active tab, the link to "undelete" a datasets flips away too quickly to use (seems to be referencing one of the copies), since it is quickly replaced the file content (seems to be referencing a different copy?). 

See screenshots. 

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
UseGalaxy.org
The Galaxy Server is running version [24.0.rc1 ](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/release_24.0/releases/24.0_announce_user.html), and the web client was built on Thursday Mar 14th 3:00:16 2024 GMT-7 .

**Browser and Operating System**
Operating System: Windows, Linux, **macOS**
Browser: Firefox, **Chrome**, Chrome-based, Safari

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
0. Load up a few datasets, and put those into a collection
1. Go to the hidden tab, notice that each of the original datasets has been duplicated (expected)
2. Set up some changes in the second set (those that are "in" the collection)
* Change the datatype to something else (user _might_ do this, I used since batch change on collection is not working and it ended up helping to learn what was going on overall https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/17734)
* Delete one or more of the datasets (what a user did by accident here https://help.galaxyproject.org/t/dataset-temporarily-displaying-as-deleted-preventing-job-from-running/11923, or maybe there were trying to manage quota directly)
3. Go back to the top level of the history, and view just active datasets, then layer in deleted and hidden
4. Notice that the collection has been updated with the new datatype assigned to the datasets (good!)
5. Click to expand the collection, and notice that the datasets still have the old datatype (seem to be referencing the originally added datasets?, not so good...)
6. Try to view the datasets in the collection using the "eye" icon to figure out the content (users do this, as do I, for first pass troubleshooting, say from a job error reporting "datasets deleted before job could run"), and notice that the dataset that was deleted in Step 2 above quickly flips between a) red bar with an undelete dataset link and b) regular file display of the contents (link is not usable, see the Ghelp ticket above for more about this situation).
7. The collection cannot be used as an input (but can be selected on some tools forms and the run fails, and some bounce as containing deleted content directly on the form -- the latter is better). 
8. Big picture: how to recover is not obvious.

**Expected behavior**
Reference/display the datasets _actually inside the collection_ in all collection related views, including display views. As part of this, if a dataset is in a deleted state, leave the "click to undelete" view as a final state and not display the file contents (even if available under the hood). When undeleted, then it can be displayed.


**Screenshots**
These are screenshots showing everything after Step 3 above
..
..
![Screen Shot 2024-03-14 at 10 55 27 AM](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/8999735/6eaa1ec8-b942-469a-ad21-8b8639b88a28)
..
..
![Screen Shot 2024-03-14 at 10 55 52 AM](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/8999735/b3a29a61-576b-4148-aff4-e10ca5e57d9e)
..
..
![Screen Shot 2024-03-14 at 10 56 23 AM](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/8999735/e6482e1c-7eee-4121-b584-a41307b120d5)
..
..
![Screen Shot 2024-03-14 at 10 56 52 AM](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/8999735/5c64efb1-5f37-4d84-94bf-98c41433fa3e)
..
..
![Screen Shot 2024-03-14 at 10 57 13 AM](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/8999735/04afe9cd-faf1-4de1-bb40-616ab0a9c0f2)
..
..
![Screen Shot 2024-03-14 at 12 01 40 PM](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/8999735/8d04cb66-d580-472b-bfd7-00a43983a9b2)

instances)"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-03-19T21:49:33Z","updated_at":"2024-03-19T21:49:33Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hi all!

I'd like to improve how hub links are labeled when they display as cross links into the [Galaxy Help](https://help.galaxyproject.org) forum.

This is an example topic at Galaxy help https://help.galaxyproject.org/t/maxquant-runs-and-stops-without-result/11961/2?u=jennaj where someone out in the wild happened to have a question about tool use locally, _and we have that tool in Galaxy_! An excellent opportunity to bridge someone into **using Galaxy** :) 

Unfortunately, I was only able to post the URL to the very top of the site and have it display as just the URL. 

So .. I envision a few improvements, and this is just a starter list. At a minimum let's at least do the first since it seems easy.

1. Improve link resolution for key areas, or at least the top level. We've done this for the GTN
2. Expand into standardize md content display for different introduction use cases when a Q comes up
3. Entry via an existing video?
4. Create a stand-alone static page at the Hub, similar to these guides under the Resources category https://help.galaxyproject.org/docs. I might make one as a draft in the meanwhile, and we can tune it up. 
5. Standardize graphics display across these entry points. This is probably possible now but I'm not sure where those graphics live in the repo or if they will display correctly in the Hub using the defaults. SOS for this please...

If there is interest, I can join a hub working group meeting to get this started, grant moderation to the people involved, etc. 


Please adjust the tags I added to this since I just guessed!

cc 
@martenson

How the problem presents:
Tool can fail when many inputs are given -- or not fail and just produce emtpy outputs, in particular the core alignment.

Possible solution (guess!):
Add in `-z` to the command string?
https://github.com/sanger-pathogens/Roary/issues/519

Alternative solutions might include:
Have the tool just warn about GFFs left out of the final result, instead of failing?
Output a better log? When the tool produces empty outputs users are confused about why, and if not "red" failure no bug report options, so may be a more common issue than we know about.

References:
* Several user issues at the developer's repo. https://github.com/sanger-pathogens/Roary/issues. Search with "proteome" or "core alignment".
* Questions at the Ghelp forum, example: https://help.galaxyproject.org/t/roary-troubleshooting-version-3-13-0-galaxy2-or-later/. More found by searching with keyword "roary" -- some were about the prior file naming issue but even once adjusted (by user or via the updated wrapper) -- the mystery about why empty results remains. 
* Older tracking ticket: https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/issues/293#issuecomment-1255461595

https://training.galaxyproject.org/training-material/topics/assembly/tutorials/mrsa-illumina/tutorial.html

Zenodo: https://zenodo.org/records/4534098

Ok --- https://zenodo.org/record/4534098/files/DRR187559_1.fastqsanger.bz2
Fails "as truncated data" with FastQC --- https://zenodo.org/record/4534098/files/DRR187559_2.fastqsanger.bz2

The second file R2 in bz2 format is tossing errors with **FastQC** at both usegalaxy.org and usegalaxy.eu.

If that same file is uncompressed but otherwise unchanged, it works fine with FastQC.

If that file is left bz2 compressed, it fails just FastQC but works with the tutorial's workflow for the other steps.

Fails "as truncated data" with FastQC --- https://zenodo.org/record/4534098/files/DRR187559_2.fastqsanger.bz2

The second file R2 in bz2 format is tossing errors with **FastQC** at both usegalaxy.org and usegalaxy.eu.

If that same file is uncompressed but otherwise unchanged, it works fine with FastQC.

If that file is left bz2 compressed, it fails just FastQC but works with the tutorial's workflow for the other steps.

Compression problem? Doesn't seem to be actually truncated.

Multi-select: odd dataset order, on tool forms and history output","user":{"login":"jennaj","id":8999735,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg5OTk3MzU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8999735?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj","html_url":"https://github.com/jennaj","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jennaj/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":180024669,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxODAwMjQ2Njk=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/kind/bug","name":"kind/bug","color":"84b6eb","default":false,"description":null},{"id":241204064,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNDEyMDQwNjQ=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/area/UI-UX","name":"area/UI-UX","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-01-17T20:16:42Z","updated_at":"2024-01-17T20:16:42Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
When using the multi-select on tool forms, input datasets added to the field are always in the reverse order of the dataset numbering. This was previously smaller > larger dataset number. Some users noticed this. Could we order that way again?

Then, the output order seem to be: _the second highest number_ to the smallest number, followed by the highest number (!!)

Yes, using a collection would solve this. But wondering if sorting small > large for both form and outputs is possible just for UI niceness, plus consistency with earlier Galaxy releases.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
UseGalaxy.org, Galaxy Version Information
The Galaxy Server is running version [23.2.rc1 ](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/release_23.2/releases/23.2_announce_user.html), and the web client was built on Wednesday Jan 17th 6:13:41 2024 GMT-8 .

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. test history: https://usegalaxy.org/u/jen-galaxyproject/h/multi-select-reverse-ordering-example
2. pick a tool and add the history datasets one at a time and you'll notice it. Higher numbered datasets are sorted to the front of the listing 
3. submit the job to see the output ordering

**Expected behavior**
Smallest > largest dataset number on both the select box and history outputs. Why? This is how it was originally.

**Screenshots**

Input select:

![Screen Shot 2024-01-17 at 12 03 17 PM](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/8999735/2b28e54f-2982-4c61-bc8e-f7ee4a66cc92)


Outputs this:

![Screen Shot 2024-01-17 at 12 07 53 PM](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/8999735/ef82edaf-f72e-4281-a144-e3381ab6223e)

**Additional context**
If someone already reported, please consolidate or merge :) Also, wasn't sure how to tag. Thanks!

This isn't for the Bioconductor team, it is for the Galaxy wrapper. Eg trap the problematic usage. 

This tool is very tricky for people to use, and is unique in that the "order" of data consumption depends on the order of datasets in the user history, not how they select the inputs on the form, and it must be the same between the two separate input types. BAM input order + BED input order == same or it fails with a scientific warning when the actual problem is mismatched order of the pairing. 

We get a LOT of bug reports for this one, and the solution is complicated. Either copy the datasets in the correct order into a new history OR create collections in the right ordering. In short, forces the user to do somewhat advanced manipulations in Galaxy that are not related to scientific goals. I can't think of any other tool that works this way. I know why it is modeled this way but maybe we can be clever and come up with something better?

I'm not stuck on how this is addressed but improving the usability would be a win.       * Optional, same as in the tool form help section. Could go over or under Troubleshooting section but always after logs and param sections.
